docs.title: Risa's All In One Menu
docs.text:
One launcher hotkey (F1) opens many mod menus — and frees each mod's own hotkey for other use.

Tired of remembering a different hotkey for every mod menu, or fighting keybind conflicts? Risa's All In One Menu brings supported SKSE mod menus together under one unified launcher, keeping your keyboard free for gameplay, other mods, and custom bindings.

  • One key for everything

    Press F1 to open the launcher, pick the menu you want. Press F1 again or Escape to close it.

  • Frees your keys

    Each supported menu's original hotkey is blocked or relocated so you can rebind it to anything else.

  • Uses official APIs

    Where a mod exposes an API, the launcher uses it for reliable opening, closing, and state tracking.

  • Clean uninstall

    A one-click restore returns every managed mod to its original hotkey before you remove the mod.

docs.title: Quick start
docs.text:
1. Install with a FOMOD-aware mod manager (see Installation).
  2. Launch Skyrim, reach the main menu, then exit and relaunch once so newly managed menus settle.
  3. In game, press F1 to open the launcher.
  4. Click a mod's button to open its menu. Press F1 again or Escape to close.

The launcher hotkey is configurable

F1 is the default, but you can change it — and add Ctrl / Shift / Alt, double-tap, and Easy Close options — from the launcher's Settings tab.

docs.title: Key features
docs.text:
• Launch supported mod menus from one interface.
  • Dedicated sub-menus for Community Shaders and Skyrim Party Sheet.
  • Rebind supported menu hotkeys directly from the Settings tab.
  • Blocks or relocates supported menu hotkeys to reduce conflicts and free those keys.
  • Uses official menu APIs where available; other integrations use direct menu control or a single targeted config edit.
  • Custom hotkeys are saved and carry over between updates and new games.

docs.title: Where to get it
docs.text:
• Source & releases: github.com/Risasre22/Risas-All-In-One-Menu
  • Nexus Mods: Risa's All In One Menu

docs.title: Files & Hotkeys Edited
docs.text:
“Original” means the value my menu captures before managing a mod. Restore for Uninstall returns captured values whenever possible; the listed upstream defaults are used only as fallbacks.

Keys Freed for You — 26 bindings using 19 distinct hotkeys

If every supported integration is installed, my menu manages 26 default hotkey bindings across 20 mods. These bindings use 19 distinct hotkeys, because several mods share F1, Home, F6, or End. MCM and Quick Armor Rebalance are also supported, but neither has a native default hotkey.

  • SKSE Menu Framework: F1
  • dMenu NG: Home
  • FLICK: F7
  • CatMenu: F6
  • Dragonborn’s Toolkit: F1
  • Debug Menu: F1
  • SearchUI: F4
  • Mod Function Menu: F1
  • Modex: Delete
  • Hotkey Reminder: F11
  • Open Animation Replacer: Shift + O
  • Immersive Equipment Displays: Backspace
  • Outfit Preview Selector: Numpad 1
  • Skyrim Vanity System: F6
  • Skyrim Party Sheet: X (Settings), F6 (Party Sheet), Y (Inspect Card), U (Character Sheet) — 4 bindings
  • ENB Editor: Shift + Enter
  • Community Shaders: End (Main), Shift + End (Editor), F10 (Overlay), Numpad * (Effects) — 4 bindings
  • Improved Camera: Shift + Home
  • ReShade: Home
  • KreatE: End

docs.title: Files my menu edits
docs.text:
Only the listed hotkey values are changed. Other settings in each file are preserved.

Open Animation Replacer Path: SKSE\Plugins\OpenAnimationReplacer.ini Default key: Shift + O Default values: uToggleUIKey = 24 | uToggleUIKeyShift = 1 | uToggleUIKeyCtrl = 0 | uToggleUIKeyAlt = 0 Changed to: uToggleUIKey = F13 | Shift/Ctrl/Alt = 0. F13 is used as an unpressable parking key.

Improved Camera SE (the NG/API path may require no file edit) Path: SKSE\Plugins\ImprovedCameraSE\ImprovedCameraSE.ini Default key: Shift + Home Default value: MenuKey = 0x24 (Home) Changed to: MenuKey is normalized to Home when needed. My menu then hides the chord only from Improved Camera, leaving Shift + Home available elsewhere.

Immersive Equipment Displays Path: SKSE\Plugins\ImmersiveEquipmentDisplays.ini Default key: Backspace Default values: ToggleKeys = 0x0E | OverrideToggleKeys = false Changed to: ToggleKeys = F16 | OverrideToggleKeys = true. F16 is used as an unpressable parking key.

Debug Menu Path: MCM\Settings\DebugMenu.ini (or MCM\Config\DebugMenu\settings.ini) Default key: F1 Default value: uOpenMenuHotkey = 59 Changed to: uOpenMenuHotkey = F17 only when it conflicts with the launcher or contains an older relocation made by my menu. Otherwise its configured key is left in the file and intercepted live.

KreatE Path: KreatE\UserSettings.ini Default key: End Default value: GUIToggleKeys = 0x23 Changed to: GUIToggleKeys = F18 (unpressable parking key).

Community Shaders Paths: SKSE\Plugins\CommunityShaders\SettingsUser.json and SettingsDefault.json when present Default keys: End / Shift + End / F10 / Numpad * Default values under “Menu”: "ToggleKey": 35 | "CSEditorToggleKey": [16, 35] | "OverlayToggleKey": 121 | "EffectToggleKey": 106 Changed to: ToggleKey = F19 | CSEditorToggleKey = F15 with no modifier | OverlayToggleKey = F23 | EffectToggleKey = F24.

Skyrim Vanity System Path: SKSE\Plugins\SkyrimVanitySystem\settings.json Default key: F6 Default value: "toggleKey" = 64 Changed to: "toggleKey" = 109 (F22 unpressable parking key). My menu injects F22 to open or close the menu and restores the captured key on exclusion or uninstall.

Modex Path: Interface\Modex\user\settings.json Default key: Delete Default value: "Open Menu Keybind" = 211 Changed to: "Open Menu Keybind" = 0 (disabled). My menu refreshes the setting through Modex's official native API and opens Modex directly. Closing uses a UI-message workaround because Modex 3.0's CloseMenu API currently reopens the menu.

Dragonborn’s Toolkit legacy fallback (v0.5+ uses its live API and does not need this edit) Path: SKSE\Plugins\SkyrimCheatMenu.json Default key: F1 Default value: "toggleKey": "F1" Changed to: "toggleKey": 0 only when the runtime hotkey API is unavailable.

ReShade Path: ReShade.ini in the game folder Default key: Home Default value: KeyOverlay=36,0,0,0 Changed to: KeyOverlay=0,0,0,0. The file disables the native key; my ReShade add-on opens and closes the overlay through the API.

docs.title: Managed live without editing another mod’s file
docs.text:
• SKSE Menu Framework: native F1 listener disabled/restored through its API.
  • dMenu NG: Home disabled/restored through the v2 API. Stock dMenu without that API is left unmanaged.
  • Photo Mode: opened and closed through po3_photomode.TogglePhotoMode(true/false). Its primary open key is disabled by default; no settings file is edited.
  • FLICK: F7 disabled/restored through its hotkey API.
  • SearchUI: F4 parked live on F18 through Papyrus.
  • Outfit Preview Selector: Numpad 1 parked live on F18 through its Papyrus hotkey property; opening uses its Papyrus method and closing uses the OPS_CloseMenu mod event; no INI is edited.
  • Hotkey Reminder: F11 listener disabled/restored through ToggleHotkey(). Its settings INI is read only.
  • Skyrim Party Sheet: X, F6, Y, and U are intercepted live; PartyUserSettings.ini is read only.
  • ENB Editor: Shift + Enter is hidden only from ENB through caller-scoped input handling.
  • Dragonborn’s Toolkit v0.5+: F1 listener disabled/restored through its runtime API.
  • Mod Configuration Menu: no native hotkey to edit.
  • Quick Armor Rebalance: no native default hotkey to edit.

docs.title: My menu’s support files
docs.text: These files belong to my menu. They are not files from another supported mod.

docs.title: Settings and backups
docs.text: File Purpose SKSE\Plugins\RisaAllInOneMenu.ini Stores my menu settings and original-hotkey options. SKSE\Plugins\RisaAllInOneMenu_OriginalHotkeys.json Stores captured settings used by Restore User Original Settings. SKSE\Plugins\RisaAllInOneMenu_OriginalHotkeys_Completed.json Archives a completed original-settings backup after a successful uninstall restore instead of deleting it. SKSE\Plugins\RisaAllInOneMenu\Translations\ Contains the language JSON files shown in the language dropdown.

docs.title: Logs
docs.text: File Purpose Documents\My Games\Skyrim Special Edition\SKSE\RisaAllInOneMenu.log Main troubleshooting log. Enable Troubleshooting Log in Settings before reproducing an issue. Documents\My Games\Skyrim Special Edition\SKSE\RisaAllInOneMenu_FileChanges.log Records each supported-mod configuration edit and restore when Log File Changes is enabled.

docs.title: Hotkey Allocation
docs.text:
To free your everyday keys, the launcher relocates some mods' listeners onto the unpressable F13–F24 keys and opens those mods itself. This page is the ledger of which of those keys are in use — useful for advanced users and for other mod authors who want to avoid clashes.

You don't need this to use the mod

Everyday users can ignore this page — F1 opens the launcher and that's all you need. This is reference material for troubleshooting and integration.

docs.title: F13–F24 key ledger
docs.text:
Key Status Owner / reason F13 Reserved Open Animation Replacer's native listener is parked here. F14 Fallback Stock dMenu (no v2 API) is parked here. With the dMenu NG v2 build the key is disabled live and Home stays free — nothing parked. F15 Reserved Community Shaders Editor listener. F16 Reserved Immersive Equipment Displays parked here; Backspace freed. F17 Free No current owner. F18 Reserved KreatE relocated listener. F19 Reserved Community Shaders main menu listener. F20 Free No current owner. F21 Free CatMenu listener is intercepted; its saved value can't react. F22 Fallback ReShade compatibility path (non-add-on only). F23 Reserved Community Shaders Overlay listener. F24 Reserved Community Shaders Effect listener.

Immediately reusable: F17, F20, F21 Conditionally free: F14 (whenever dMenu NG v2 is in use), F22 (if ReShade's non-add-on fallback isn't needed)

docs.title: Keys freed for your use
docs.text:
Because the launcher takes over these menus, the mods' default keys are freed back to you:

  • Home — freed (dMenu, when the NG v2 build is used)
  • Backspace — freed (Immersive Equipment Displays)
  • Shift+Home — freed (Improved Camera; blocked only from IC, so still usable elsewhere)

docs.title: Keys still driven by a hotkey
docs.text:
A few integrations still work through a real (relocated or native) key rather than an API:

Mod Key(s) Notes dMenu (stock only) F14 Used only when the dMenu NG API is absent. KreatE F18 Relocated window/ImGui input listener. Community Shaders F15, F19, F23, F24 Four separate menu actions. Debug Menu F3 Managed key outside the F13–F24 pool. ENB Editor Shift+Enter Native chord, caller-scoped. ReShade (non-add-on) F22 Compatibility fallback only.

docs.title: Escape behavior
docs.text:
F1 opens and closes the launcher; Escape closes the active mod menu. While a menu that would otherwise let Skyrim's journal/pause pop is open (Debug Menu, ENB, KreatE, Dragonborn's Toolkit, CatMenu), Escape is intercepted so only that menu closes — the vanilla menu won't appear.

Why modifier keys don't multiply the pool

Ctrl / Shift / Alt don't automatically create more free keys — some mods ignore modifiers and react to the base key. A chord is only reusable once that listener has been tested or isolated.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Skyrim SE / AE with SKSE64
  • SKSE Menu Framework (the launcher renders through it)
  • Any of the supported mods you want to control (all optional)

docs.title: Installing
docs.text:
Install with a FOMOD-aware mod manager (Mod Organizer 2, Vortex). The installer has one step:

  1. Main plugin — always installed.
  2. Optional: dMenu NG (API build) — an add-on dmenu.dll that adds live hotkey control to dMenu NG, so the launcher can free the Home key with no ini edit and no restart. Offered only if it's useful to you; the original dMenu, dMenu NG, and the included build are all supported.

The optional dMenu build is not required

If you don't use dMenu, skip the optional add-on. If you do, it's auto-checked when dMenu is detected. Big thanks to C0kAdam for making that collaboration possible.

docs.title: First launch
docs.text:
Some mods only create their config after running once

A number of supported mods generate their configuration files only after they've been launched once. When you install a newly supported menu mod:

  1. Launch Skyrim once so that mod's config is created.
  2. Launch Skyrim with Risa's All In One Menu enabled, reach the main menu, exit, and relaunch.

Integrations that use an official API usually work immediately without this extra restart.

docs.title: Load order
docs.text: Risa's All In One Menu doesn't replace any menus and has no hard conflicts with the mods it manages. Standard SKSE plugin placement is fine. If you use the optional dMenu build, let it win over the original dMenu / dMenu NG plugin.

docs.title: Availability of some buttons
docs.text:
Main-menu vs in-game

Blacked-out buttons — such as Debug Menu and ENB — are unavailable from Skyrim's main menu and become available after loading a save.

docs.title: Integration Status
docs.text: This page shows how my menu integrates each supported menu and how each original hotkey is handled.

docs.title: Legend
docs.text:
• Gold letters = perfect integration
  • ✅ = update-resilient integration
  • ⚠️ = compatibility update may be needed
  • ▶️ = direct open/close through a non-key method
  • ⌨️ = key freed live with no file edit
  • 💾 = key freed through INI/JSON/config editing
  • ✏️ = API request pending or historically requested from the original mod author
✅ ▶️ ⌨️ SKSE Menu Framework Native hotkey listener managed through its API. ✅ ▶️ ⌨️ Mod Configuration Menu Opens directly to SkyUI's configuration panel. ✅ ⌨️ F18 SearchUI Hotkey managed live through Papyrus; no INI edit. ⚠️ ▶️ 💾 ✏️ F13 Open Animation Replacer Direct open is version-dependent. ⚠️ ▶️ 💾 ✏️ F16 Immersive Equipment Displays Direct open is version-dependent. ⚠️ ▶️ ⌨️ ✏️ F4 Debug Menu Intercepted live; its INI is changed only when a launcher-key collision requires it. ✅ ▶️ ⌨️ dMenu NG Requires the optional API build. Uses the v2 API to open, close, and disable or restore Home live. No INI edit. ⚠️ Improved Camera SE 1.1.2 Not supported. ✅ ▶️ ⌨️ Improved Camera NG 2.0.0.1215 Opens through Improved Camera's registered ic menu handler; no INI edit. This version is available through Discord, not Nexus. ✅ ▶️ ⌨️ FLICK Native listener managed through its API. ⚠️ 💾 ✏️ F18 KreatE Uses its settings file. ⚠️ 💾 ✏️ x4 Community Shaders F15 / F19 / F23 / F24 ✅ ▶️ ⌨️ Dragonborn's Toolkit Native listener managed through the runtime API. ✅ ▶️ ⌨️ CatMenu Optional API build opens and closes directly and manages the native listener live. No JSON edit and no parked F-key. ⚠️ ▶️ 💾 ReShade The add-on API opens the overlay; Home is disabled in ReShade.ini. ⚠️ ▶️ ⌨️ ✏️ Skyrim Party Sheet Native keys are intercepted live with no INI edit. ✅ ▶️ ⌨️ Quick Armor Rebalance Opens through Papyrus. It has no native default hotkey. ✅ ▶️ ⌨️ Photo Mode Opens and closes through Photo Mode's built-in Papyrus API. No settings file is edited. ⚠️ ▶️ 💾 ✏️ Modex Opens through its official API; Delete is disabled in its settings file. ✅ ▶️ ⌨️ Hotkey Reminder Public API controls the listener with no settings edit. ⚠️ 💾 ✏️ F22 Skyrim Vanity System F6 is parked through settings.json. ✅ ▶️ ⌨️ Mod Function Menu Optional API build opens and closes directly and controls only its native opening listener. No TOML edit and no parked F-key. ✅ ▶️ ⌨️ Outfit Preview Selector Uses Papyrus and a mod event; its native hotkey is managed live.

docs.title: Supported Mods
docs.text: The launcher opens each mod's own menu — it never replaces them. Where a mod offers an official API, that's used for reliable open/close/state tracking; otherwise the launcher uses a verified direct-open path or a single targeted config edit. Unrelated settings in a mod's config are preserved.

docs.title: Supported menus
docs.text:
Mod How it's integrated Notes SKSE Menu Framework API (keyless) Required — the launcher renders through it. Open Animation Replacer Direct open Opens through its own UI manager; native key parked. Immersive Equipment Displays Direct open Opens via its native render task; Backspace freed. Debug Menu Managed key Driven through a managed hotkey. dMenu / dMenu NG API (keyless) With the optional NG API build, Home is freed live — no restart. Improved Camera SE / NG Command Opens via ic menu; Shift+Home freed for others. ENB Editor Managed chord Native Shift+Enter chord, caller-scoped. FLICK API (keyless) Native key disabled via its official API. KreatE Relocated key Listener moved off its default key. Community Shaders Managed keys Has its own sub-menu (main / editor / overlay / effect). Dragonborn's Toolkit API (keyless) Official open/close/is-open interface. CatMenu Direct open Native listener intercepted. ReShade Add-on API (keyless) Overlay driven through the ReShade add-on API. Skyrim Party Sheet Input sink (keyless) Has its own sub-menu; no hidden keys reserved.

Sub-menus

Community Shaders and Skyrim Party Sheet each open a dedicated sub-menu inside the launcher, so their multiple actions are all reachable from one place.

docs.title: Original hotkeys
docs.text:
Supported menu hotkeys are managed by the launcher so they don't open their menus accidentally. You can turn any individual original hotkey back on from the Settings tab.

When an original hotkey is enabled, it can open and close its menu even if the mod's internal key has been reassigned by the launcher.

See a mod's original + managed key

Hover over a hotkey setting in the Settings tab to see the mod's original hotkey and the internal key currently assigned by my menu. For the full technical ledger of which keys are reserved, see Hotkey Allocation.

docs.title: Compatibility
docs.text: This mod does not replace any supported menus — it opens the original menus and manages their hotkey behavior. Official APIs are used where available; other integrations use direct menu control or configuration-based hotkey management, and unrelated settings in supported config files are left untouched.

docs.title: Uninstall & Restore
docs.text: Because the launcher manages other mods' hotkeys, you should let it put those hotkeys back before you remove it. This is a one-click step in the launcher.

docs.title: Before uninstalling
docs.text:
1. Open the launcher (F1) and go to the Settings tab.

  2. Choose one of the restore options:

    • Restore My Original Settings — reverts each managed mod to the value it had before my menu first changed it. Missing captured values fall back to the mod's safe default.
    • Restore Supported Mod Defaults — sets each managed mod to its upstream default hotkey, ignoring any captured custom value.

  3. When restore finishes, choose to quit to desktop (or exit Skyrim normally).

  4. Disable or remove Risa's All In One Menu before launching Skyrim again.

The restored hotkeys take effect on the next game launch.

Disable the mod before relaunching

If you relaunch Skyrim with the mod still enabled, it will re-apply its hotkey management. Restore → quit → disable the mod → then launch again.

docs.title: What restore touches
docs.text: Restore only rewrites the specific hotkey values the launcher itself changed, in each managed mod's config. Unrelated settings in those files are left alone. Files that don't exist are skipped.

docs.title: If restore reports "incomplete"
docs.text:
If a restore reports that it was incomplete, it means one config file genuinely couldn't be written (for example it was read-only or locked). Every other mod is still restored, and the originals backup is kept so you can fix the cause and run restore again. Check Documents\My Games\Skyrim Special Edition\SKSE\RisaAllInOneMenu.log for the specific file.

Audit log

A second, plain-English log — RisaAllInOneMenu_FileChanges.log — records exactly which config files the mod edits (EDIT) and reverts (REVERT), and nothing else. Both logs can be toggled in the Settings tab.

docs.title: Fresh start
docs.text: After a successful restore, the originals backup is removed so a future reinstall starts clean and captures your settings fresh.
